name: Pulumi Python SDK (Infrastructure as Code)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); Pulumi Cloud from $0 (free tier) to enterprise

auth.notes: Pulumi access token for state backend; provider credentials (AWS, GCP, Azure)

performance.notes: Infrastructure deployment; speed depends on provider API and resource count

agent_readiness.notes: Infrastructure as code with real programming languages. import pulumi, pulumi_aws as aws. bucket = aws.s3.Bucket('my-bucket'). server = aws.ec2.Instance('web', instance_type='t3.micro', ami=ami_id). pulumi up deploys. State management via Pulumi Cloud, S3, or GCS. 60+ providers (AWS, GCP, Azure, K8s). Automation API: pulumi.automation for programmatic stack management. Testing: pulumi.testing mock provider. Compared to Terraform: real loops, functions, classes.

gotchas: Python async patterns don't work inside Pulumi (special Output type)
Pulumi Output type requires .apply() for chaining values
State backend required (Pulumi Cloud free tier or self-managed S3/GCS)
Large stacks can be slow to update (provider API rate limits)
best_when: Infrastructure as code with real Python loops, functions, classes
Teams preferring Python over HCL for infrastructure
Programmatic stack management via Automation API
avoid_when: Teams with existing Terraform expertise (migration cost high)
Simple infrastructure (Terraform's declarative HCL often clearer)
When Terraform provider ecosystem coverage is critical
summary: Pulumi Python SDK provides infrastructure as code using real Python. import pulumi_aws as aws; aws.s3.Bucket('my-bucket') — real Python loops, functions, classes vs Terraform HCL. 60+ providers. State via Pulumi Cloud or S3/GCS. Automation API for programmatic stack management. Testing with mock provider. Output type for async chaining. Best for teams preferring Python over HCL for infrastructure definition.
